_note: Synthetic example demonstrating U₀ failure mode. 16 LED_PANEL_600 panels clustered in central 4.5×4.5 m of a 10×8 m room; perimeter rows missing. INV-01 + INV-02 FAIL HIGH; INV-03..09 PASS. 10-sample slice from the 99-point grid; full grid emitted by runtime calc.lumen_grid_solver. Lumen-method-alone trap: room-average 478 lux is within 5% of 500 target, but spatial distribution catastrophically fails per-point + U₀.

consumed_summary._layout_note: 16 panels clustered at (x, y) ∈ {2500, 4000, 5500, 7000} × {2500, 4000, 5500, 7000} — central 4.5×4.5 m area; perimeter 2 m strips on all 4 sides lack any luminaires (this is the spatial-distribution defect under test)

ies_files._source: Synthetic 600x600mm recessed LED panel, 6000lm, 4000K, opal diffuser. verification_status: synthetic_reference_C3 — generated by scripts/generate_reference_ies.py. Used to demonstrate U₀ failure mode from clustered-grid spatial distribution that lumen-method INV-1 alone misses.

grid_metadata.grid_spacing_formula: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §6.2 adaptive: p = 0.2 × 5^log₁₀(d) clamped [50, 1000] mm; for d=9.0m task area: p ≈ 0.2 × 5^0.954 × 1000 ≈ 922 mm → snapped to nominal 900 mm. Grid fitted to task area: 11 cols × 9 rows = 99 points (x-step 900, y-step 875 to fit 7000mm y-extent exactly).

reflectances._source: Default open_plan_office reflectances per CIBSE LG7 §6.2 clean office (0.7/0.5/0.2)

non_compliance_flags.message: Achieved min illuminance 150 lux at SE corner (8600, 7500) fails BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 requirement: per-point min must be ≥ 0.7 × target 500 = 350 lux. Spatial cause: 16 panels are clustered in central 4.5×4.5 m area; perimeter 2 m strip is unlit. Recommend redistributing panels into a uniform 4×5 grid (matching D3 canonical) OR adding 4 perimeter panels to break up the cold-corner deficit.

Achieved uniformity U₀ = 150/478 = 0.31 fails the 0.6 open_plan_office target per BS EN 12464-1 §4.4. The cluster-and-perimeter-cold-spot pattern produces 6× ratio between max (908 lux at cluster centre) and min (150 lux at corners). Engineer-of-record must redistribute luminaires before the lighting-layout can pass full_drawing sign-off.
non_compliance_flags.reference: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 uniformity requirement + Table 5.3 row 5.34

rationale.chat_summary: Synthetic U₀ failure demo: 16 LED_PANEL_600 clustered in central 4.5×4.5 m of 10×8 m office. achieved_avg 478 lux (4% below 500 target — already a marginal lumen-method result), but min 150 lux at corners fails BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 per-point 0.7× threshold (350 needed) and U₀ 0.31 fails the 0.6 office target. INV-01 + INV-02 FAIL HIGH. Demonstrates the spatial-distribution failure mode lumen-method alone cannot detect; photometric-analysis catches it structurally. UGR still PASSes (clustering doesn't drive glare disproportionately).

sections.title: Cascade context
sections.summary: Consumes synthetic upstream lighting-layout intent (./synthetic-upstream-intent.json) — not a real lighting-layout example; constructed to exercise the U₀-FAIL path of INV-01 + INV-02. Demonstrates how photometric-analysis structurally catches what room-average lumen-method INV-1 misses.

sections.title: Grid resolution derivation
sections.summary: Same 9×7 m task area as Example 1. d=9m; §6.2 corrected formula p ≈ 922 mm → 900 mm. 11×9 = 99 grid points. The grid is uniform; the failure is in the LUMINAIRE placement (clustered), not the evaluation grid.

sections.title: Per-point illuminance
sections.summary: achieved_avg 478 lux (-4% vs 500 target — slim margin even at avg level). achieved_min 150 lux at corners NW/NE/SE/SW (4 corners equally cold due to clustering symmetry). achieved_max 908 lux at room centre (under 4 panels). 6× ratio between hot centre and cold corners is the classic clustered-grid signature.

sections.title: UGR per CIE 117
sections.summary: Max UGR 17.6 (auto_S_wall) ≤ 19 office limit; INV-03 PASS. Clustering DOESN'T drive glare disproportionately — UGR depends on luminaire-to-eye geometry per CIE 117, and the perimeter observers still see roughly the same set of luminaires from similar angles. UGR is uniformity-blind.

sections.title: Engineering signal
sections.summary: Engineer-of-record sees task_area_compliant=false propagated upstream to lighting-layout INV-11; D-1 reviewer flag (D-1 BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 per-point failure) fires. Required action: redistribute the 16 panels into a uniform 4×5 or 4×4 grid covering the full task area, OR add 4 perimeter panels to break the cold-corner deficit. After re-run, photometric-analysis emits task_area_compliant=true.

sections.title: Honest disclosure
sections.summary: Synthetic upstream intent + synthetic IES file; this example is a skill self-test demonstration of the U₀-FAIL path, not a real-project case. The intent shape mirrors the real lighting-layout intent contract; the clustered layout is the contrived defect under test.

invariants.evidence: Achieved min 150 lux at SE corner (8600, 7500) < 0.7 × target 500 = 350 lux. FAIL by 200 lux (57% deficit). Per BS EN 12464-1 §4.4 + Table 5.3 row 5.34 open_plan_office. Lumen-method INV-1 would not have caught this (avg 478 is within tolerance band of 500 target).

Achieved U₀ = 150/478 = 0.314 < 0.6 office target per [ugr-rules#per-room-type-limits] open_plan_office. FAIL by 0.286 (48% deficit). The clustered-grid spatial distribution produces 6× max-to-min ratio (908 at centre vs 150 at corners) — far worse than typical 4× ratio that a uniform grid produces.

Max UGR 17.6 (auto_S_wall observer at (5000, 1500)) ≤ 19 office limit per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.3 row 5.34. UGR is geometry-driven (observer-to-luminaire angles) not distribution-driven; clustering doesn't break UGR even when it breaks U₀.
1 distinct luminaire_type (LED_PANEL_600) in upstream synthetic intent consumed_summary; 1 ies_files[] entry matches; LM-63 parser returns total_lumens=6000 + max_intensity_cd=1850.

grid_spacing_mm=900 matches BS EN 12464-1 §6.2 formula output p ≈ 922 mm within ±50 mm tolerance. d=9.0m task area (unchanged from Example 1; the grid is a function of room geometry, not luminaire placement).
illuminance_grid.length (full runtime emission) = point_count=99. 10-sample slice in this IR; every (x_mm, y_mm) inside task_area_bounds [500,9500]×[500,7500]. No duplicate coordinates.
4 ugr_results entries with _source=cie_117_default (N, S, E, W wall-facing per [ugr-rules#default-observer-positions]). No engineer overrides supplied.
